item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No idea what Pitch Haven is, but this was a very enjoyable read.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it comes from here under the same license.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A very good work, I enjoyed it.

+1

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: timer expired, edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This SCP is interwoven quite a bit with the lore of Isaac, who is not only a fairly major player in the Pitch Haven series (SCP-2746 is already linked here), but is also a pivotal character in SCP-2988. I propose linking that to here for that reason.

Editing this:

This is so exciting! As of today I am now under the apprenticeship of Healer Izaak!

To this:

This is so exciting! As of today I am now under the apprenticeship of Healer Izaak!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These are some of my favorite multiscp works

item.title: Foreboding. . .
item.content:encoded:
So this is who SCP-990-02 spoke of in their warning…
Wake-Up Call

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been a pretty big fan of the entire mythos regarding Sarah Crowley and others. I love what you've added as I actually had to check to see if Fantem was the writer since the quality was pretty good! To be honest, this entire series is what got me to create an account and attempt to write a few drafts of my own! In any case, I really do love this one as well. I am curious though as to whether or not we will ever see what is inside that hyena statue.

Additionally, I am curious whether or not Nao's body has finished being created yet? Would I be correct to assume that she is creating a human body?

In any case, from one Japanese person to another:

よくがんばったね！

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yah. Doing research into your cultures is the best way to get SCP fuel . :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Being Japanese, I am ashamed to say that I had done no prior research on spiders in my culture, nor did I have any knowledge of the Jorogumo while writing this. ._.

But now that I know, I have to say there are so many things about these yokai that Nao can relate to.

I should implement this and make it canon for an upcoming Tale I'm writing. Thank you for pointing this out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: is this realeated to the Jorōgumo of japanese folklore?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for responding, that all makes sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for catching those! I'll fix accordingly.

+ Regarding some of the points... :)
- Hide

In point 3, remember that these are written entries; after seeing your friend torn apart, you probably won't be thinking too much about proper grammar. ;P

In point 5, he's already lost his leg, Nao just sewed up that stump; when she says she can't get it back, I meant to mean that even if they did have the leg, it couldn't be reattached. JJust miscommunication on my part part, sorry.

In regards to the translated text, I understand that Fantem uses some sort of cipher/coding to make the new words; not every translated symbol corresponds to a single letter, so that's probably why there's lowercase letters mixed in. (To be honest I don't fully know how she does it myself ._.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall I like this article and think it is a good addition to Pitch Haven.

I feel there that there are a few small issues in the journal logs, which I don't want to edit myself since they involve the A-12 translations.

Issues?
Issues?

1. Tab 1, close to the end of the translation, there appears to be a stray >.

2. Tab 3, "We saw blood on some the buildings nearby." Should there be an "of" before "the?"

3. Tab 3, the repeated "she's dead" does not include an apostrophe.

4. Tab 3 and 4, the translated all capital text sometimes includes lowercase letters.

5. Chronology error? Did Izaak visit Nao after he made the tree? If so, his leg was attached to the tree so the following statement would already be evident. "I was able to close the wound but there's no hope in getting it back completely."

Thanks for looking over these comments, I feel that the article could be improved by these changes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No problem, I get what you're saying. I do understand what you mean by the mythos being confusing to those who aren't familiar with it, though personally I find that when I read an SCP that relates to another it makes me want to dive into the universe even more to get the whole story; the first thing that comes to my mind is the Herman Fuller SCPs, which have been another one of my big inspirations for writing around here. (That being said, most of those are independent of each other overall, with individual experiences of performers/items, rather than an interconnected story.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I figured that this deserved another look now that it has been posted and had time to simmer down. As I said during the review process, I really liked the rose spiders, and the concept of them serving as workers for a larger purpose. That’s creepy and really draws me in. Unfortunately, upon being drawn in, I find that the article really bogs itself down in the Pitch Haven mythos and eventually lost me, which to me stands as a bit of a problem.

I think building a canon off SCP articles is a dandy idea; however, I am of the opinion that main list articles are not tales in a series. They should not be so heavily based off one another that to read one article without having read the others would leave the reader lost.

I really liked the concept for this SCP, but I’m going to no vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neet.

It sure is convenient for the Foundation that 80% of anomalous beings take copious notes about everything they do and like to leave them laying around in easy to find places.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ^_^ Thank you, Fantem! Always fun to write for this storyline!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The addition to the description and the transcripts definitely clarifies things. Can't wait to hear more about Izaak's following. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a person. But it's spiders? But it's a person. anyway +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tenouttaten.

A lovely read and I'm proud to have it as an addition to the canon. Upvoted with the force of a thousand spider gods.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: spideeerrrrrssss ;^; +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Spiderfeels, spiderfeels, does whatever spiderfeels does… +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I edited that before I saw your post thinking it was a typo.

Also +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. It is possible that these other journals are hidden within SCP-X; retrieval of these is considered a high priority task for personnel studying SCP-X.

Is this meant to be 'SCP-X' or 'SCP-2905' or something else?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A thoroughly enjoyable read.

Upvoted.

item.title: New Pitch Haven Addition
item.content:encoded:
Here's the latest addition to the Pitch Haven universe! Really excited to be posting a new thing!

As usual, shoutouts!

Special thanks to SunnyClockwork, Conwell, hihahaba, Algorithm, Accelerando, and Fingo7 for reviewing 2905 in draft stage.

As always, huge thanks to Fantem for creating the canon, reviewing the draft as well, and providing the original A-12 translations in the story!

Hope you enjoy!
